channel.title: InsurTech Intelligence
channel.description: Noticias globales de insurtech, actualizadas cada 6 horas.

item.title: «Hay que tener referentes, pero también saber contar nuestra propia historia»
item.description: En esta línea, destacó el papel de los referentes, pero con un matiz claro: “Hay que tener referentes, pero también saber contar nuestra propia historia, porque todos tenemos una historia detrás”, puntualizando la importancia de darnos valor. Uno de los ejes clave fue el mentoring , que definió como “un espacio donde analizar nuestra carrera, lo que queremos y también aquello que nos frustra”. Asimismo, lanzó un mensaje directo sobre el desarrollo profesional: “Tenemos que dar el paso, apostar por nuestra carrera y por las habilidades que tenemos, no por las que nos faltan.

item.title: Jóvenes que rompen tópicos
item.description: Son iniciativas que promueven el esfuerzo, la constancia y la superación; proyectos que permiten al sector asegurador conectar con los jóvenes y ser percibido de una manera distinta, más cercana y más humana. Hay apuestas que, para las aseguradoras, terminan siendo algo más que una simple acción de difusión de marca: se convierten en una forma de poner en valor su papel en la sociedad y en la vida de las personas. Un ejemplo claro es el testimonio de Jaime Mencía Úbeda-Portugués: Ayer, domingo 26 de abril, crucé una meta que, hace no tanto, parecía lejana, casi imposible: 42 kilómetros.

item.title: Zinnia Adds FIA Chassis Zahara Platform
item.description: Zinnia (Greenwich, Conn.) has added a preconfigured fixed indexed annuity chassis to its Zahara policy administration platform, designed to support faster product development and launch for life insurers. “Zahara is a cornerstone of our platform and reflects our focus on delivering real outcomes for carriers,” says George Esposito, CEO, Zinnia. By starting with a pre-built chassis, carriers can iterate earlier, embed testing throughout development, and accelerate the path from design to launch.” Zinnia says the platform is designed to support interoperability with existing carrier systems and includes capabilities for digital distribution, compliance and lifecycle management.

item.title: The Enterprise Playbook for Human-Powered Claims
item.description: In Wisedocs’ recently released guide “Rebuilding Trust in AI: Using Human-Assisted Intelligence for Enterprise Claims Transformation”, it reveals why the insurance industry lags in AI adoption and how forward-thinking carriers can gain a competitive advantage through human-assisted intelligence. Ultimately, these missteps left enterprise carriers and claims organizations with broken promises — and a clear need for a more practical, defensible path forward. View Series The insurance industry is currently facing a paradox: while 78% of workers across all industries use AI in some capacity, only 42% of claims professionals have embraced this technology .

item.title: India’s Acko targets second-half IPO at up to US$2.5bn valuation: reports
item.description: To view the full article and get unlimited access to InsuranceAsia News's exclusive insight across 5,000+ articles

item.title: Aon promotes Lee Huang Ng to chairperson for Taiwan
item.description: To view the full article and get unlimited access to InsuranceAsia News's exclusive insight across 5,000+ articles

item.title: FCA Board appoints 2 new members to the Regulatory Decisions Committee
item.description: The Board of the FCA has appointed Jonathan Peddie and Raymond Cox KC as new members of the FCA’s Regulatory Decisions Committee (RDC) . The FCA Board appoints new members to decision-making committee. Both Raymond and Jonathan bring significant legal and financial services expertise and regulatory enforcement experience, which will complement existing committee members and enhance the overall capability and effectiveness of the RDC.'

item.title: FCA consults on changes to IPO research rules
item.description: It also consults on removing rules that require firms to provide independent analysts with the same information as their own research analysts. The FCA is seeking views on proposals to change rules that govern the publication of research during the initial public offering (IPO) process. These rules were introduced in 2018 to encourage the production of unconnected research, but they have not achieved that aim.

item.title: Exclusive: Goldman Sachs Leads $60M Series C For Personal Loan Fintech Kashable
item.description: Kashable , a fintech that provides access to “socially responsible” credit and financial wellness programs for employees as a voluntary benefit, has secured $60 million in a Series C funding round led by Goldman Sachs Alternatives ’ Sustainable Investing. Goldman Sachs Alternatives has committed up to $50 million to the round, including an initial $25 million investment and an additional $25 million to be funded in the coming months, subject to undisclosed conditions. Existing backers Revolution and EJF Ventures also participated in the financing, which brings New York-based Kashable’s total equity and debt raised to more than $450 million since its 2013 inception.

item.title: WFP progresses food security catastrophe bond plan, to mobilise $100m for drought resilience
item.description: The United Nations World Food Programme (WFP) is progressing a plan to become the first to utilise catastrophe bonds for food security financing, in a novel transaction that would see it become the first UN agency to sponsor a cat bond, with a target to source up to $100 million in risk transfer to boost drought resilience in Sub-Saharan Africa. This drought catastrophe bond, dubbed a food security catastrophe bond by the WFP, would be groundbreaking for a number of reasons. The UN World Food Programme has been exploring catastrophe bonds under its Innovation Accelerator initiative for around two years now, but the project is becoming increasingly formalised with service providers such as insurance-linked securities experienced law firms now being on-boarded to assist with legal structuring in the development of a drought cat bond, we understand.

item.title: Cayman considers catastrophe bond, more parametric insurance to expand hurricane protection
item.description: As the 2026 Atlantic hurricane season approaches, the government of Cayman is considering expanding its hurricane protection by turning to the insurance-linked securities (ILS) market to potentially issue its own catastrophe bond, while simultaneously expanding its parametric insurance coverage too, Cayman Compass reports. As Artemis’ readers are aware, there are two key insurance options available to Cayman, one of which would be for the government to issue its own catastrophe bond. Another option is to increase Cayman’s coverage in CCRIF SPC, formerly known as the Caribbean Catastrophe Risk Insurance Facility, a regional catastrophe fund that provides specialised, parametric insurance to Caribbean and Central American governments.

item.title: Kinsale Capital reports improved Q1’26 underwriting income
item.description: has reported stronger underwriting income of $94.5 million and a combined ratio of 77.4% for the first quarter of 2026, compared to $67.5 million and 82.1%, respectively, for Q1’25. The increase in underwriting income was driven by growth in net earned premiums of $407 million as compared to $366 million in Q1’25, lower catastrophe losses, and higher favourable development of loss reserves from prior accident years. Kinsale’s net income for Q1’26 rose to $112.6 million, compared to $89.2 million in Q1’25.

item.title: Fathom Re appoints Mark Rex as Managing Partner
item.description: Dallas, Texas headquartered reinsurance intermediary, Fathom Reinsurance Partners, has announced the appointment of industry veteran Mark Rex as Managing Partner. “Mark plays a key role in the firm’s strategic direction and oversight of key initiatives, with a focus on business development and the continued evolution of its technology and analytic platforms,” a Fathom Re spokesperson commented. His arrival is expected to significantly enhance Fathom Re’s client advisory capabilities, particularly in program design and strategic risk transfer.

item.title: Bermuda and US re/insurers maintain strong capital positions despite rising losses and softening market: Fitch
item.description: Fitch Ratings, a global credit rating agency, has published a report reviewing a group of Bermuda and US-based re/insurance companies, namely Arch Capital Group Ltd., Ascot Group Limited, Hamilton Insurance Group Ltd., RenaissanceRe Holdings Ltd. Based on Fitch Ratings’ Prism Global Capital Model, capital adequacy for the group was assessed as ‘Very Strong’ to ‘Extremely Strong’ at year-end 2025. Fitch expects underwriting results to face continued pressure in 2026 as market conditions soften, although all companies are still expected to maintain underwriting profitability.

item.title: Iran War Disrupts the Circuit Board Supply Chain, Raises Costs for Tech Firms
item.description: The disruption is a fresh blow to electronics manufacturers which are already grappling with soaring memory chip costs and highlights the broadening impact of the Iran war that has wreaked havoc on supply chains, plastics, and oil supplies. The conflict in the Middle East has disrupted supplies of crucial raw materials and pushed up prices of the printed circuit boards (PCB) used in almost all electronic devices, from smartphones and computers to AI servers, industry sources and executives said. Iran struck Saudi Arabia’s Jubail petrochemical complex in early April, forcing a halt in production of high-purity polyphenylene ether (PPE) resin — a critical base material used to manufacture PCB laminates.

item.title: Hormuz Shipping Traffic Remains Muted With no US-Iran Deal in Sight, Data Shows
item.description: At least seven ships – mainly dry bulk vessels – have crossed the Strait of Hormuz in the past 24 hours, in line with muted activity in recent days, shipping data showed on Monday, while talks between Iran and the United States have stalled. Shipping traffic passing through the crucial waterway at the entrance to the Gulf during an uneasy ceasefire between Washington and Tehran represents a fraction of the average 140 daily passages before the Iran war began on February 28. The vessels included ships leaving from Iraqi ports and one dry bulk vessel from an Iranian port, according to ship tracking data from Kpler and separate satellite analysis from data analytics specialists SynMax.

item.title: King Bars Policy Cancellations in Georgia Counties as Fires Spread from Helene Debris
item.description: King’s April 23 directive bars cancellations for nonpayment of premiums in 91 of Georgia’s 159 counties, where fires this month have forced more than 200 residents to flee. Georgia insurance commissioner John King used his statutory authority to bar property insurance policy cancellations until further notice, due to wildfires that have displaced homeowners across the state. “There’s a ton of old Hurricane Helene debris down in the woods,” said Seth Hawkins, a Georgia Forestry Commission spokesperson.

item.title: The Hartford leans into small business
item.description: The Hartford reported another strong quarter in Business Insurance, with written premiums up 6% and an underlying combined ratio of 89.2%. Small business stood out, delivering 8% premium growth and a combined ratio of 89.4%, driven by double digit gains in package and commercial auto. Management highlighted growth into the small end of the middle market, the ability to shift risks from admitted to E&S, and broader product bundling.

item.title: State Farm files trademark for MEER
item.description: trademark application for “ MEER ,” signaling a potential move around data-driven capabilities in insurance. No description or product details were included in the filing, and there’s no indication yet of how “MEER” will be used—whether as an internal analytics platform, customer-facing tool, or broader brand. The application, submitted on April 22, 2026, covers “financial data analysis in the field of insurance” and sits under International Class 036, which includes insurance and financial services.

item.title: RLI starts 2026 strong, stays disciplined as E&S softens
item.description: RLI Insurance Company opened 2026 with an 86% combined ratio and 3% premium growth, while net investment income rose 15%. Results were slightly impacted by catastrophe losses, but the underlying business remains stable. Premiums grew 10%, with personal umbrella up 23% and rates increasing 16%.

item.title: Hiscox on Tour busca impulsar el talento en la Mediación
item.description: Mañana arranca en Madrid la cuarta edición de Hiscox on Tour, una gira impulsada por la aseguradora centrada en la gestión del talento en las corredurías. Bajo el lema ‘Impulsando el talento en la Mediación’, la iniciativa de Hiscox ofrecerá pautas para identificar, desarrollar y fidelizar equipos. La sesión inaugural será a las 11.00 horas en la Fundación Giner de los Ríos e incluirá una formación de Growz sobre la evolución del talento, los perfiles más demandados y los retos del canal.

item.title: Europa acelera la transformación financiera con foco en resiliencia digital y sostenibilidad
item.description: El Comité Europeo de Supervisores (EBA, Eiopa y ESMA) ha publicado su informe anual de 2025 en el que destaca la digitalización, la resiliencia cibernética y las finanzas sostenibles como principales prioridades del ejercicio. Durante el pasado año, el Comité centró su actividad en reforzar la protección de los consumidores “en un entorno financiero cada vez más digitalizado”, así como en fortalecer la resiliencia operativa y frente a ciberataques mediante la implementación del Reglamento DORA. El informe también recoge avances en diversas iniciativas transversales , como el refuerzo del marco europeo de titulizaciones, el progreso del European Single Access Point (ESAP) y el impulso a la innovación financiera a través del European Forum for Innovation Facilitators.

item.title: Aunna reúne en Tenerife a más de 200 profesionales con el foco de innovar desde las raíces
item.description: Aunna Asociación ha celebrado una nueva edición de su Espacio & Asamblea 2026, que tuvo lugar del 22 al 24 de abril en Tenerife bajo el lema ‘Innovar desde nuestras raíces’. El presidente de la asociación, Santiago Macho, presentó las novedades de Aunna Tech, la plataforma de gestión en la que ya están migrando los socios. Clausuró el evento destacando la importancia de impulsar la innovación sin perder las raíces.

item.title: Study: ‘Alarming’ High Flood Risk for 17 Million on Atlantic, Gulf Coasts
item.description: Atlantic and Gulf coasts are at the highest risk of being affected by flooding, with New York and New Orleans standing out, according to one of the most comprehensive studies ever of flood risk. The study found 4.3 million people along the coasts to be at the highest level of risk of extreme flooding, but 20.5 million to be at high risk, the second highest level. They then brought in past damages from the Federal Emergency Management Agency’s database and applied three different artificial intelligence tools to figure out flood risks from Texas to Maine, calculating that 17.5 million people were at “very high” risk and an additional 17 million were at “high” risk, the next level.

item.title: Report: Cargo Theft Down for Quarter, But Criminals Are Getting More Savvy
item.description: and Canada in the first quarter of the year, down 5.3% from the same period a year ago and down 12.2% the fourth quarter of 2025. Most of the top states for cargo theft saw year‑over‑year declines. The report shows this was driven by cargo thefts of cosmetics and fragrances, predominantly in the Northeast.

item.title: Mitsubishi Motors Recalling 108,000 US Vehicles Over Liftgate Issue
item.description: Related: Jaguar Land Rover Recalling 170,000 US Vehicles Over Loss of Drive Power As a remedy, Mitsubishi will replace the left and right liftgate gas springs, free of charge, the auto regulator said. Mitsubishi Motors is recalling 108,046 vehicles in the U.S. as a ruptured gas spring cylinder or an unexpected falling liftgate could increase the risk of injury, the U.S.

item.title: Insurance’s Data Problem Comes Into Focus at Hormuz
item.description: In recent weeks, the Strait of Hormuz has forced the marine insurance market into real-time decision making. Over the past several years, the insurance industry has invested heavily in artificial intelligence, particularly in tools that extract structured data from unstructured documents. Rapidly shifting war-risk conditions are exposing structural weaknesses in delegated authority underwriting, according to Alex Babin, CEO of Hercules Systems, who notes that many underwriters still rely on delayed, inconsistent bordereaux data.

item.title: ICA calls on Australian government to establish US$21.6bn flood defence fund
item.description: To view the full article and get unlimited access to InsuranceAsia News's exclusive insight across 5,000+ articles

item.title: Best of Artemis, week ending April 26th 2026
item.description: Ten most read articles on Artemis.bm, week ending April 26th 2026: This is not every article published on Artemis during the last week, just the most popular among our readers over the last seven days. Here are the ten most popular news articles, week ending April 26th 2026, covering catastrophe bonds, ILS, reinsurance capital and related risk transfer topics. To ensure you never miss a thing subscribe to the weekly Artemis email newsletter updates or get our email alerts for every article we publish.

item.title: Louisiana Citizens targets slightly lower pricing for $150m Bayou Re 2026-1 cat bond
item.description: Returning earlier this month with this Bayou Re 2026-1 catastrophe bond deal, it marked the seventh year in a row that Louisiana Citizens has sought to sponsor a new cat bond. These Bayou Re Series 2026-1 cat bond notes will provide Louisiana Citizens with a capital markets backed and collateralized source of reinsurance against named storm losses on an indemnity trigger and per-occurrence basis, across a three-year term to the end of April 2029. Louisiana Citizens Property Insurance Corporation continues to target $150 million of named storm reinsurance from its twelfth catastrophe bond sponsorship, while the price guidance for the Bayou Re Ltd.

item.title: Tributary Public Risk begins operations with specialist cover for Canada’s public sector
item.description: Tributary Public Risk (TPR), a newly established insurance provider focused exclusively on public sector organisations, has formally commenced operations as the first insurer domiciled in Canada designed specifically for this market. “Public sector organisations face exposures that differ fundamentally from commercial risks,” commented Chris Lorne, Chief Executive Officer of Tributary Public Risk. The company was created to address the distinct risk, governance and procurement requirements of municipalities, schools, Crown agencies and other public bodies.

item.title: IMA Financial Group places $4bn risk programme for AI-data centre company
item.description: IMA Financial Group, a North American insurance brokerage firm, has completed a $4 billion property insurance placement on behalf of a publicly traded AI and high-performance computing (HPC) data centre company. The firm’s specialisation in data centre risk began in the early 2000s, allowing them to develop the technical fluency and sector expertise essential for today’s demanding AI and HPC facilities. IMA Digital Risk Practice Leader Patrick Datz, said: “This placement reflects two decades of experience in the data centre space, bringing that expertise to today’s most advanced facilities.

item.title: Miller grew again in 2025 with total revenues of £314m
item.description: Gross written premium of $4 billion is in line with the previous year, although total revenues increased by 15% to £314 million in 2025, compared with £271 million in 2024 , as Miller delivered its fifth consecutive year of growth. Independent specialist insurance and reinsurance brokerage, Miller, placed around $4 billion in total gross written premium during 2025, as 180 new people joined in a milestone year for the company. In June 2025, Miller completed its acquisition of AHJ Holdings Ltd.

item.title: Commercial Lines Rates Continue to Soften, Says Ivans Index
item.description: Premium renewal rates in the first quarter 2026 decreased for all major commercial lines compared to the fourth quarter 2025, according to the Ivans Index. “ Q1 sets an important baseline for 2026, with commercial rates continuing to soften as the market adjusts to a more moderate pace of change ,” said Michael Streit, president of Ivans. Highlights by line of business, according to the Ivans Index : Topics Commercial Lines Business Insurance

item.title: NFL’s Rooney Rule Meets Biggest Challenge in Trump’s DEI Crackdown
item.description: Florida Attorney General James Uthmeier, who has been endorsed by President Donald Trump, is calling for the end of the National Football League’s Rooney Rule, a provision that requires teams to interview at least two minority candidates for head coaching jobs. The most-recognizable diversity policy in sports is facing the biggest challenge in its history. The NFL has considered changes to the Rooney Rule in response to the DEI backlash before deciding against them, said two people familiar with the league’s deliberations.

item.title: Michigan Cleared in Lawsuit Over 2020 Dam Collapse
item.description: The state of Michigan was cleared of liability Thursday in a disastrous 2020 dam failure that flooded communities, destroyed more than 100 homes and drained a popular lake. Lawyers representing thousands of people sued the state, claiming decisions by environmental regulators contributed to the dam collapse and ruined property values and their enjoyment of Wixom Lake, 150 miles (241 kilometers) northwest of Detroit. The dam was in the process of being turned over to area residents when the failure occurred, releasing a torrent that also overtopped the downstream Sanford Dam and flooded the city of Midland.

item.title: Directivos financieros coinciden: colaboración sectorial es clave para enfrentar riesgos crecientes de ciberseguridad
item.description: La colaboración en materia de ciberseguridad y prevención del fraude también refleja un mayor nivel de madurez, de acuerdo con Sergio Arias, presidente de Grupo Covalto y director general adjunto de Banco Covalto. Con la inteligencia artificial elevando el nivel de sofisticación de los fraudes, tanto internos como externos, las instituciones financieras coinciden en que la cooperación entre la industria y las autoridades será determinante para hacer frente al desafío. Así lo señalaron directivos durante el FinSeg Day 2026, organizado por iupana, en Ciudad de México.

item.title: Wall Street Law Firm Apologizes for AI ‘Hallucinations’ in Court Filing
item.description: Sullivan & Cromwell, a premier Wall Street law firm, apologized to a federal judge for submitting a court filing with inaccurate citations and other errors generated by artificial intelligence. In a letter dated April 18, Andrew Dietderich, co-head of the firm’s global restructuring group, said the errors included AI “hallucinations” – instances in which AI makes up case citations, misquotes the law or generates non-existent legal sources. The letter did not say what AI program was used to help produce the court filing.

item.title: ‘The Arms Race Is On’: Chubb’s Greenberg on Mythos, Middle East
item.description: Greenberg said the “arms race is on.” Speaking during a conference call to discuss first quarter earnings , Greenberg told analysts Anthropic’s Mythos artificial intelligence model has lowered the threshold for vulnerability. When it comes to Anthropic’s Mythos as friend or foe, Chubb CEO Evan G. The offense is “just around the corner,” Greenberg said to give analysts a sense of how Chubb is being thoughtful about the topic.

item.title: NY to Lose $74M of Federal $ Over 33,000 Immigrant Trucker CDLs
item.description: New York will lose more than $73.5 million in federal money because the Transportation Department said last week that state has refused to revoke nearly 33,000 questionable commercial driver’s licenses for immigrants since an audit uncovered problems last year. The federal government has reviewed records related to these non-domiciled CDLs in every state since Transportation Secretary Sean Duffy put a spotlight on this issue after an August crash in Florida that killed three people. The department said that more than half of the 200 licenses reviewed during the audit had significant problems such as remaining valid long after an immigrant was authorized to be in the country.

item.title: Moody’s upgrades Peak Re’s financial strength rating to A3
item.description: To view the full article and get unlimited access to InsuranceAsia News's exclusive insight across 5,000+ articles

item.title: Swiss Re faces backlash for underwriting new LNG terminals, CEO defends ‘very robust’ ESG framework
item.description: To view the full article and get unlimited access to InsuranceAsia News's exclusive insight across 5,000+ articles

item.title: ‘Not much more to give’: Asia’s D&O rate softening may moderate in 2026 amid geopolitical tensions, AI rollout
item.description: To view the full article and get unlimited access to InsuranceAsia News's exclusive insight across 5,000+ articles

item.title: Maine Governor Blocks First State Freeze on New Data Centers
item.description: The Democratic governor of Maine, Janet Mills, on Friday vetoed a bill that would have made it the first U.S. state to impose a moratorium on large new data centers, even as local opposition to the electricity-hungry facilities grows. If signed into law, the bill would have frozen approvals until October 2027 for data centers requiring more than 20 megawatts of power while a state-appointed council analyzed ⁠their impact on the local grid, electricity bills, air and water.

item.title: Long-Term Impact of Today's Oil Crisis
item.description: A year‑long oil crisis would hit both automobile sales and auto insurance in ways that go far beyond just higher gas prices. Unfortunately, that is just not realistic, and the longer-term damage is already set in motion. When fuel becomes expensive for a long period, consumers rethink what they drive.

item.title: Nationwide launches ClearAccess tool for self-funded employers
item.description: Nationwide is introducing ClearAccess, a healthcare navigation tool designed to help employers manage costs and improve the member experience within its Self-Funded Program. Packaging the ClearAccess tool with Nationwide’s Self-Funded Program means members are getting a clearer, more affordable care experience.” – Casey Lanto, head of Nationwide Group Benefits Product and Operations. Nationwide’s Self-Funded Program targets employers with 2 to 500 employees, offering an alternative to fully insured plans.

item.title: W. R. Berkley names new head of Berkley Environmental
item.description: Berkley names new head of Berkley Environmental appeared first on Coverager - Insurance news and insights . Christopher DeLauder as president of Berkley Environmental , effective immediately. DeLauder has nearly 40 years of experience in environmental insurance and joined Berkley Environmental in 2010, most recently serving as executive vice president.

item.title: Socotra CEO Warns Against ‘AI Demo Theater’
item.description: Dan Woods, CEO, Socotra, does not lack for conviction when it comes to the insurance industry’s current fixation on artificial intelligence. In a recent conversation with Insurance Innovation Reporter , he offered a view that cuts against the prevailing momentum: much of what carriers are seeing today is not production-grade capability, but performance. A bullfighter, labeled “IT vendor,” stands poised with a red cape emblazoned “Vibe-Coded AI Demo,” as a charging bull labeled “Insurance Carrier” bears down.

item.title: Underwriting, unbundled by AI
item.description: To view the full article and get unlimited access to InsuranceAsia News's exclusive insight across 5,000+ articles

item.title: World Insurance Associates acquires Baldon Insurance Group
item.description: World Insurance Associates acquired Baldon Insurance Group , a Patchogue, NY-based commercial insurance agency, effective January 1, 2026. The deal adds another regional agency to World’s network as it continues to expand its footprint and capabilities across commercial insurance. Founded in 2000, Baldon focuses on commercial clients, including hospitality, real estate, and construction businesses.
